core_mechanism: conditional-independence factorization of a joint probability measure into a product of conditional marginals (screening), tested by multiplicative separation of a Radon-Nikodym density between the measure and a reference law, with the residual coupling quantified by the Kullback-Leibler divergence between the joint law and the product of its conditional marginals (conditional mutual information)
brief_reason: The abstract's central question is when the conditional law of histories factorises, with the boundary property becoming multiplicative separation of a Radon-Nikodym derivative and the failure of factorisation measured by conditional mutual information (relative entropy between the true conditioned law and the product of its conditional marginals).
